Now you can use reusable Harmonized Document Management (HDM) attachment UI adoption in the Change Maintenance Notification web dynpro application. If you choose in the menu the Manage Attachments option as shown in the next video, a new window will open with the SAP Fiori HDM attachment UI. Here you can upload or assign documents or add links. If you upload for instance a picture, a new Document Info Record (DIR) is created and the uploaded picture is uploaded to the Maintenance Notification.

Video 6: 'Change Maintenance Notification' app - Harmonized Document Management (HDM) Attachment

(🔙Table of Contents)

Resource Scheduling 

Work Center Utilization Calculations shown in User Time Zone

Previously work center utilization was calculated based on the system time zone (UTC), which did not allow
users working in other time zones to see the data in a meaningful way, especially with maintenance operations that are planned in night shifts. The data is now displayed according to the user’s specific time zone. This applies to the following apps: 

  • Resource Scheduling for Maintenance Planners
  • Manage Work Center Utilization
  • Maintenance Scheduling Board

Watch the next video that shows how the Operation Planned Start and Operation Planned End date and time are displayed according to the user’s specific time zone.

Video 7: Work Center Utilization Calculations shown in User Time Zone

(🔙Table of Contents)

SAP Maintenance Assistant 

Install and Dismantle Equipment

SAP Maintenance Assistant is the mobile maintenance execution solution for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Watch the next video to learn the improvements to user experience in SAP Maintenance Assistant app and the new capability to install and dismantle equipment.

Video 8: SAP Maintenance Assistant - Install and Dismantle Equipment

This demo was presented by Zack Frazier during the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series. Check out also Zack's brand new blog post SAP Maintenance Assistant 2408 Now Available – What’s New?.

(🔙Table of Contents)

 

SAP Asset Performance Management 

SAP Asset Performance Management empowers asset owners, plant managers, and reliability engineers to optimize maintenance strategies, measure and improve asset performance, and follow through on your sustainability objectives. Kate Dowle shares the latest news around SAP Asset Performance Management in her blog post series. Check out her brand new blog post What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2408 that provides videos on our latest updates, including Joule's AI-powered integration with SAP Help Portal (part of the Early Adopter Access initiative) and enhanced features like global operating contexts in strategy assessments for classes, and time-saving enhancements to FMEA assessments.

(🔙Table of Contents)

 

More Information / Additional links 

You will find a collection of SAP S/4HANA Cloud product centric blog posts from Markus Oertelt and me here:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management – The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Manufacturing & Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • Discrete Industries in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - The Link Collection

 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X. 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series! 

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a xx-minute-long recording and a presentation for LoB/Industry/Topic as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below: 

 

(🔙Table of Contents)

entry.title: Asset Revaluation & Acquisition in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition via SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
This blog is intended to complement my previous blogs on automation and augment Finance Asset Accounting scenario with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition as described with available scope items for Asset Revaluation (J62) and SAP Help documentation

A Professional Services company wanted to streamline its operations, particularly in managing the intricate logistics of end-to-end fixed asset acquisition and posting asset revaluation processes. 

Company discovers asset revaluation with seamless automation. By effortlessly collecting data from Asset Accountants through various document formats, including PDFs, we streamline the entire process.

In comprehensive approach, company identify assets acquired both in the current fiscal year and previous fiscal years. Each revaluation includes meticulous details such as Ledger information, Fixed Asset Revaluation Acquisition Amounts, Current Year Amounts, and the increments by which the original acquisition costs (APC) are adjusted.  In addition, specific details around corresponding cumulative value for ordinary revaluation are included.

SAP Build Process Automation's low-code solutions, designed to empower users to create and manage processes and workflows effortlessly. With the no-code process builder, enhancing enterprise applications and business sites is as simple as drag-and-drop.

 

Automation for asset accounting is driven by SAP Build Process Automation Actions (API), and rapidly developed UI-based automations for both SAP and third-party applications using the built-in automation recorder.

Decision management capabilities transform corporate policies and business rules into repeatable processes. The intuitive business rules editor lets users create decision tables, similar to spreadsheets, or text-based rules.

To streamline access and enhance operations, SAP Build Process Automation offer a unified launchpad and task center, providing a seamless user experience. This allows business users to easily access and manage notifications and tasks from various solutions, whether via web or mobile.

In a custom scenario evaluated by the Professional Services company, SAP Build Process Automation demonstrates how it can simplify process and task automation for business users without any coding required. The platform's built-in AI capabilities facilitate intelligent document processing, effortlessly extracting data from both structured and unstructured documents, and seamlessly transferring it to your enterprise systems.

In specific customer business use case, SAP Build Process Automation enhance accuracy by using document templates instead of relying solely on pre-trained models. This is achieved by uploading the business document to the service and creating a document template artifact, which can extract data from various file types (e.g., PDFs) as required for the specific business case.

Company evaluates the SAP Build Process Automation to elevate the efficiency and precision of their Asset management business operations, providing a robust solution for intelligent document processing and seamless integration with enterprise systems.

Enterprise Automation with SAP serves as the foundational layer for integrating Generative AI into your business processes. By embedding AI models into orchestrated workflows and task automations, you can significantly enhance the level of automation and efficiency in your operations. You can learn more with roadmap items

In future enhancements, customer plan to explore the benefits of AI-powered artifact creation and recommendations, areas not covered in the current scope.

  • Generative AI will streamline the creation of process and rules artifacts, leading to more efficient process analysis, automation, and integration capabilities.
  • AI-powered tools will generate integration flows that support seamless scaling and address deviations through advanced anomaly detection.

You can stay ahead of the curve by leveraging the latest AI advancements to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of your business operations.

Furthermore, Event-Driven Architectures with SAP Build Process Automation empower business users to fully automate processes in response to business events. (see Business Accelerator Hub for more details).  You can utilize existing business event objects or design custom ones to monitor and subscribe to specific occurrences within your system of records, proactively triggering automations.

SAP Build Process Automation seamlessly integrates Document Information Extraction capabilities, effortlessly extracting relevant data from both structured and unstructured documents, such as scans.

Key data points, including asset acquisition date, useful life, business transaction type, and debit/credit indicators, are captured to facilitate year-end closing and the display of transactions in the asset history sheet.  It adeptly recognizes whether a revaluation is an increase or a reduction, and accurately extracts Revaluation Amounts for both APC (Acquisition Costs) and Ordinary Depreciation.

Leveraging AI-based document processing, SAP Build Process Automation enhances document extraction with features like automatic template detection and prebuilt activities for efficient data extraction. The system also can support data enrichment, like linking asset and company information from templates to match codes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, and can automatically align text.

This AI-driven approach to document information extraction and recommendations underscores customer’s commitment to improving their end-user experience and maintaining a competitive edge in the market.

Consider a typical business scenario where automating asset revaluation and verifying asset posted transactions in specific ledger journal entries is essential for an Asset Accountant.

Using SAP Build Process Automation's tasks, it becomes reality to leverage API “Fixed Asset – Post Asset Revaluation” (API_FIXEDASSETREVALUATION_G4BA) to streamline the revaluation process for multiple assets in a single request. This not only enhances data accuracy but also reduces costs compared to manual revaluation entries.

API “Fixed Asset – Post Asset Revaluation”

The automation action task accesses the API, incorporating nearly all fields from the Fiori app “Fixed Asset – Post Asset Revaluation” (F7775), allowing Asset Accountants to re-valuate assets for selected ledgers and depreciation areas. Revaluations can be applied to acquisition and production costs (APCs) and ordinary depreciation amounts.

To re-valuate an asset, specify the revaluation amount by which the original APCs are to be increased and the corresponding cumulative value for ordinary depreciation. The system then posts the increased APC and ordinary depreciation for the selected ledgers and depreciation areas.

Acquisition and Production Costs encompass any asset acquired or purchased externally, including the invoice price and associated expenses such as customs, octroi, and freight. By factoring in these additional costs, you can determine the total acquisition cost essential for the capitalization of the asset.

Learn more about this efficient automation process by exploring the SAP Help API Documentation.

Next, the Asset Accountant wants to validate the revaluation postings with a list of journal entries and understand the depreciation applied to each asset using an explanation of the depreciation key configuration.

SAP Build Process Automation enables the automation of complex scenarios with ease using the recorder. By automatically capturing the "Manage Fixed Asset" application, it allows for the simultaneous design of automations, leveraging enhanced recording features. These features include recording applications combining screens on SAP GUI for Windows and UI automation technologies, enabling a seamless session that includes both Windows and Web (including UI5) interfaces.

Using a UI-based automation bot scheduled at the desired business frequency, the bot navigates to the “Manage Fixed Assets (New)” Fiori App for specific assets with revaluated postings. The bot executes UI-based navigation with a scheduled job to generate and export the list of journal entries for revaluation postings. This list includes details such as Asset Transaction Type, Ledger, Line Item, Posting Date, Valuation Date, and references, which are then emailed to business users including the Asset Accountant.

Additionally, the bot emails the Asset Accountant and defined business users an AI-assisted explanation of the Depreciation Key Configuration. This provides a better understanding of how depreciation keys operate and the rationale behind the system’s complex depreciation calculations for the specific asset.

An AI-powered, user-friendly explanation of depreciation keys facilitates quicker asset onboarding, enhances business user productivity in tracking asset value throughout its lifecycle and revaluation postings, and streamlines periodic activities. This efficiency reduces the time required to understand depreciation and asset values, driving overall operational effectiveness.

AI-assisted Explanation of Depreciation Key Configuration  (J62 - Beta) 

This AI powered feature is available only  BETA Customers & Partners and you can  find more information, on how  Get Involved Early and register @ Influence portal and experience the latest AI features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and influence upcoming AI developments.

In this business use case, the company provides assets, such as laptops and ergonomic office furniture, to consultants and project resources. Asset Accountants face the ongoing challenge of entering newly acquired assets and their inventory numbers and IMEIs into the system before distribution and activation for depreciation.

To address this, the company evaluates and customizes the SAP Build Process Automation Bot template to suit the specific needs of Asset Accountants for mass asset acquisition and creation.

The Asset Accountant uploads a pre-filled template to trigger an approval workflow, sending a notification to the Asset Manager for review. Upon the Asset Manager's approval, the automation posts the acquisition of assets in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. The Asset Accountant then receives a detailed report.

Mass Fixed Asset Acquisition SAP Build Process Automation Template

Additionally, SAP Build Process Automation template content streamlines the posting of asset acquisitions by fetching existing unposted fixed assets from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system into a template file, which users update with the necessary asset information. The updated file is then used to extract asset data and utilize the Fixed Asset Acquisition APIs to post the assets. This automation not only saves time and reduces manual errors but also improves overall efficiency. For more details, refer to the documentation on the  SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

As the company continues to acquire new assets, it must create and maintain main and sub-asset numbers on an ongoing basis. To support this, SAP Build Process Automation includes API-driven action tasks to facilitate the creation of main asset number (Learn more about Fixed Asset - Create Main Asset (Asynchronous) with API Documentation, create an sub asset Fixed Asset - Create Sub Asset (Asynchronous) (Learn more with API Documentation)

In specific scenarios, Asset Managers need to update existing assets to reflect changes in master records and valuation information.

SAP Build Process Automation’s API-based Action Task automates these updates, including modifications to the company code, main asset number, asset sub-number, origin information, account assignment, ledger details, valuation data, and time-based valuation information. This streamlined process ensures accuracy and efficiency. For more details, refer to the Fixed Asset - Change (Asynchronous). API documentation .

For more information about the extensibility of the Manage Fixed Assets app, see App Extensibility: Manage Fixed Assets (Documentation)

Visit the SAP Business Accelerator Hub to learn more about 3900+ prebuilt integration scenarios, 1300+ business events, over 250+ connectors to SAP and non-SAP applications, 1200+ automation content, and 35+ integrations all in one place, the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Stay tuned for more updates in the next quarters!

Follow us via @sap and #S4HANA, or myself via@har1234 (Hardeep Tulsi)

Stay tuned for more updates in the next quarters!

 Where to Find More Information:

  • Watch the replays of our exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/watch-the-replays-of-our-exclusive-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2408/ba-p/13757250
  • Explore the ready-to-run cloud ERP here: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/s4hana.html

  • Become an early adopter for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition here: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3612

  • Review the PPL content here: https://go.support.sap.com/roadmapviewer/#/group/658F507A-D6F5-4B78-9EE1-0300C5F1E40F/roadmapContent...

entry.title: Efficient Management of CBC Type Transport Requests in S/4HANA Cloud Public Cloud
entry.content:
Introduction

In the landscape of SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC), it’s essential to understand how to identify and manage transport requests, especially when changes are made to scoping, organizational units, or business configuration content. This blog will guide you through the steps to effectively handle these transport requests in an SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment, specifically for 3SL systems.

What is a Transport Request?

A transport request is a mechanism in SAP that facilitates the movement of changes made in one system (development) to another (testing or production). In the case of CBC, transport requests are automatically generated when deploying a workspace. These requests document all modifications, allowing for a structured approach to change management.

Understanding Transport Requests in CBC

When you deploy a workspace from the CBC, the system automatically generates a Central Business Configuration (CBC) Type Transport Request. This request captures various changes, such as:

  • Scoping adjustments
  • Modifications to entities
  • Business configuration content alterations

Key Features of CBC Transport Requests:

  • Automatic Creation: Generated during workspace deployment.
  • Automatic Release: Tasks are released automatically post-implementation.

Finding CBC Transport Requests

To locate and manage these transport requests, follow these steps:

  1. Access the Export Customizing Transports App: This app provides a centralized interface for managing transport requests.

  2. Filter by Request Type: Use the "Type" option to specifically filter for requests labeled as "SAP Central Business Configuration." This will help you focus on relevant transport requests.

  3. Check Task Status: Ensure that all tasks associated with the CBC requests have a "released" status. Only requests in this state can be processed further.

Handling CBC Transport Requests

Taking Ownership of a Request

To manage a CBC transport request, you can take ownership by clicking the Assign To Me button. This grants you the necessary permissions to release the request once all associated tasks are complete.

Releasing Customizing Requests

You can release customizing requests using the Export Customizing Transports app. It’s important to note that all tasks within requests of type CBC are automatically released after implementation, ensuring a smooth transition of changes.

Monitoring CBC Staging Status

Transport requests of type Central Business Configuration are linked to a CBC Staging ID. To check the staging status and gather related information, select Display CBC Staging in the transport request overview. This will direct you to the CBC staging area, providing more detailed insights.

If you encounter issues during staging, such as errors or activation failures, it’s essential to report an incident under the component X4-CBC-CW for assistance.

entry.title: Two-tier ERP with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Cloud Edition Overview
entry.content:
This blog aims to define two-tier ERP with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition and provide details about pre-delivered content that supports different lines of business.

Two-tier ERP overview

Two-tier ERP addresses groups of companies including headquarters and subsidiaries. It comprises a headquarters ERP with high landscape complexity and being highly customized, and a subsidiaries ERP to support quick scale at lower costs with low landscape complexity. Both headquarters and subsidiaries ERP are integrated in a hybrid landscape with suitable implementation and process optimizations.

Two-tier ERP integration content covers connecting SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as shown in the image below . Integration content included covering critical and many end-to-end scenarios and can be activated on-demand subjected business requirements. To know more click on " What is two-tier ERP? " page on sap.com


Two-Tier ERP Setup

Two-tier ERP integration between headquarters on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and subsidiaries running on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition might have different needs based on customer preferences

Two-Tier ERP Overview

Let us understand 3 different levels of integration in details

  1. Loosely coupled process – It can address requirements that can be executed in two-tier ERP set up without the real need of system-to-system integration connectivity. For example check the blog on Group Reporting  for more information 
  2. Master Data integration – It is very important to have master data integration specially product master and business partner in both the system . For example check the blog on Master Data Integration  for more information
  3. Tightly coupled process – It can address requirements  that can be executed in Two-tier ERP set up by leveraging SAP delivered communication scenario . For example sales office scenarios

From a functional scope perspective, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition offers pre-delivered two-tier ERP integrations that cover finance, order-to-cash, and procurement needs of organizations, including those in Professional Services and Manufacturing. These integrations orchestrate end-to-end processes connectivity between connected systems.

To understand how requirements can be categorized into analytics, master data, and process capabilities in a Two-tier ERP with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, let's consider the scope of order-to-cash-related requirements

The image above displays the relevant list of scope items available in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that could address two-tier ERP order to cash related scope .

However, users can explore the given path to understand the full range of SAP-delivered two-tier ERP capabilities  SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and visit

accelerators > Implementation> Two-Tier ERP_End-to-End Business Processes for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 

You can also reach out two-tier ERP community for more clarifications

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Central Business Configuration 2409
entry.content:
With the latest release for SAP Central Business Configuration, exciting new features are available. This blog post will provide you with a comprehensive overview of these features.

Improved Configuration Activities Filtering Based on Country Relevance

In the Configure Your Business Processes app, a new filter allows you to screen configuration activities based on their significance for specific countries or regions in scope. For example, it’s now possible to filter configuration activities that are relevant to one or multiple countries/regions in scope or globally. This functionality allows you to quickly identify the right configuration activities, thereby saving you valuable time.

Screenshot of the Country/Region filter in the Configure Your Business Processes app

Enhanced User Experience Within Change Logs

In the Change Logs app, we’ve enhanced the search functionality: The Changed By column now includes a filter where you can enter a username. This enhancement helps you quickly pinpoint changes made by a particular user, thereby saving you valuable time. Note that case sensitivity is not an issue as the search is not case-sensitive.

Screenshot of the Changed By column filter in the Change Logs app

For a quick summary, check out this video about What's New in 2409. Don’t forget to also use the What’s New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal, which provides further details and links about all the recent changes.

That wraps up the new features this time. Make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have an idea on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the SAP Customer Influence portal to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: Sales Office scenarios in Two-Tier ERP
entry.content:
Reflecting on the recently concluded Two-Tier ERP Confluence event, where we focused on integration and various sales office scenarios, this blog will bring a business perspective to the following key sales office scenarios and their significance:

  • Sale from Local Sales Office (Goods and Basic Services)
  • Sale from Central Stock, Direct Delivery to Customer from HQ
  • Returns Handling at Sales Office, with Credit and Debit Memo
  • Sale from HQ, Direct Procurement from Local Sales Office
  • Services from Sales Office for HQ
  • Installation, Warranty, Repair from Sales Office
  • Predictive Maintenance with AIN from Sales Office

Now, let’s take a deeper look at the different business scenarios, particularly focusing on sales from local offices.

A local sales office scenario is a prime example of loosely coupled scenario, which has been highlighted in the Two-Tier ERP Overview. In contrast, other sales office cases often involve dependent subsidiaries, which can be referred as tightly coupled scenario. You can find the relevant info here in the Two-Tier ERP Overview. Regardless of the structural differences between loosely coupled and tightly coupled, most of these scenarios can be grouped into two main categories:

  1. Inventory Sales and Returns Management
  2. Sale of services

Among the scenarios, listed above, the sale from the local sales office is well-known, but understanding why this scenario is crucial from a business perspective requires a deeper dive. This scenario enables regional sales offices to operate autonomously, driving localized decision-making, faster customer response times, and greater flexibility in meeting market demands. It is particularly beneficial when dealing with different compliance requirements, currency, or language variations across regions.

The Drop-Ship Scenario: An End-to-End Perspective

Let's now turn our attention to one of the most common sales processes from End-to-End perspective is “sale from central stock with direct delivery to the customer from headquarters”, commonly known as Drop-Ship scenario. This process offers significant advantages in maintaining lean inventories at sales offices while ensuring customer satisfaction with direct deliveries.

The following is an End-to-End scenario that offers detailed process information, outlining where each step is executed within a business context.

Future Exploration

In the upcoming blogs, we will take a deeper dive into each of the process steps outlined in the End-to-End scenario, exploring different variants and how they can benefit the business.

We will also take a deeper look in to Returns Management, in future blogs.

Sale of Services in a Two-Tier ERP Deployment

As outlined earlier, in Fig 1, the sale of services is another key scenario. For the sale of services, as outlined above, local subsidiaries manage both customer interaction and service delivery, allowing for greater agility and a better understanding of local needs. The Two-Tier ERP deployment model enables seamless integration between the local and HQ systems, ensuring smooth data flow across the organization.

For more information on warranty management, you can refer the blog here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/warranty-claim-process-in-headquarter-subsidiary-model-for-two-tier-erp/ba-p/13743032

You can also reach out two-tier ERP community for more clarifications

entry.title: PDM Newsletter GROW with SAP - October 2024
entry.content:
Welcome to the GROW with SAP Newsletter

We are thrilled to bring you the GROW with SAP Newsletter, your essential source for keeping up with the latest insights, trends, and innovations in the world of SAP. Each issue is carefully curated to provide you with expert advice, success stories, and practical content aimed at empowering you expanding your skills and thriving in the dynamic SAP landscape.

Whether you're eager to dive into new technologies or seeking tailored knowledge, our newsletter is your one-stop destination. We also encourage you to share this valuable resource with your colleagues and become an active member of our thriving community.

Thank you for your unwavering support! If you have any thoughts or inquiries, please don't hesitate to reach out. We're dedicated to helping you maximize your Grow with SAP experience.

Stay engaged, stay informed, and subscribe today by sending your details to beatriz.mata@sap.com

Let's grow together!

 

Beatriz Mata Chacón

beatriz.mata@sap.com 

Partner Delivery Manager EMEA South

SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Ecosystem Delivery Success | Partner Ecosystem Success

Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Release

The second major release of the year 2024 is here! The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release is filled with innovations that boost your productivity and support your everyday work life.

We are thrilled to give you further insights on our fully-fledged cloud ERP, supporting your journey to the intelligent, sustainable enterprise in this blog post.

This is a 3-day event, each day is 2.5 hours, and will include the following topic areas related to the finance line of business:

Partner Learning Series: Optimizing SAP Portfolio in the Public Sector

The Partner Learning Series is tailored to elevate the learning experiences of our partners seeking to maximize the use of the SAP Portfolio in the Public Sector. Our on-demand sessions, each lasting approximately 20 minutes, provide a deeper understanding of transitioning government customers to the Cloud in collaboration with experienced industry experts. You'll have the flexibility to learn at your own pace about SAP's Strategy, priorities, and trends, including SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and cloud deployment options.

The series delves into SAP S/4HANA Cloud, RISE with SAP, GROW with SAP, and SAP Business AI within the Public Sector industry, with a focus on grants and funds management, SAP SuccessFactors HCM, sourcing and procurement, and citizen services. Join us as we explore new insights and strategies to drive success in the Public Sector.

This workshop will start with an introduction to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Expertise Services Community, and we will take you through the fundamental aspects of data migration and the migration cockpit for SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

It will introduce the migration cockpit, data readiness, cutover as using the 3 system landscape for your data migration workstream. In the end we will also share some lessons learned on data migration and provide some space for question and answers.

Webinar: SAP Project and Resource Management for GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (deep dive)

After the training you will know how SAP Project and Resource Management integrates with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Professional Services scenario. How project staffing is done, which apps are provided and the key capabilities. Further you will learn which integration scenarios are available and how to proceed to setup your integration scenario..

  • Session 1: 9th October 2024, 02:30 PM - 05:00 PM Asia/Singapore

 

Webinar: Explore GROW with SAP Entitlements – SAP Ariba Buying, SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

GROW with SAP entitlements offer support for customers looking to quickly and predictably adopt cloud ERP with continuous innovation. SAP Ariba Buying, base edition creates a smart and simple purchasing experience that increases user engagement. SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management on SAP Business Technology Platform is seamlessly integrated to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and provides a single point of access and visibility to supplier invoices from multiple connected systems. Learn more about them in this session

Webinar: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Integration & Extensibility Fundamentals

This webinar will start with an introduction to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud expertise services community, outlining the importance Integration & Extensibility in SAP Activate methodology, integration types and extensibility types – followed with an opportunity to interact and raise questions.

Welcome to the Implementation Essentials event by Expertise Services on the topic of Sourcing & Procurement. This session is to support Partner project teams preparing for or involved in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementations.

entry.title: Efficient Management of CBC Type Transport Requests in S/4HANA Cloud Public Cloud
entry.content:
What is a Transport Request?

A transport request is a mechanism in SAP that facilitates the movement of changes made in one system to another. In the case of CBC, transport requests are automatically generated when deploying a workspace. These requests document all modifications, allowing for a structured approach to change management.

Identifying CBC Transport Requests

When you deploy your workspace from CBC, the system creates transport requests categorized as CBC type. These requests capture essential changes, including:

  • Scoping adjustments
  • Organizational unit modifications
  • Business configuration content updates

Finding CBC Transport Requests

To locate and manage these transport requests, follow these steps:

  1. Access the Export Customizing Transports App: This app provides a centralized interface for managing transport requests.

  2. Filter by Request Type: Use the "Type" option to specifically filter for requests labeled as "SAP Central Business Configuration." This will help you focus on relevant transport requests.

  3. Check Task Status: Ensure that all tasks associated with the CBC requests have a "released" status. Only requests in this state can be processed further.

Handling CBC Transport Requests

Taking Ownership of a Request

To manage a CBC transport request, you can take ownership by clicking the Assign To Me button. This grants you the necessary permissions to release the request once all associated tasks are complete.

Releasing Customizing Requests

You can release customizing requests using the Export Customizing Transports app. It’s important to note that all tasks within requests of type CBC are automatically released after implementation, ensuring a smooth transition of changes.

Monitoring CBC Staging Status

Transport requests of type Central Business Configuration are linked to a CBC Staging ID. To check the staging status and gather related information, select Display CBC Staging in the transport request overview. This will direct you to the CBC staging area, providing more detailed insights.

If you encounter issues during staging, such as errors or activation failures, it’s essential to report an incident under the component X4-CBC-CW for assistance.

Important Notes

  • No Task Creation: For CBC type requests, task creation is not permitted.
  • Release Requirement: All tasks within a CBC transport request must have a "released" status before proceeding. If any tasks are not released, please report an incident under X4-CBC-CW.

entry.title: Localization and Identity & Access Management (IAM) with SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408
entry.content:
Hello and welcome to this blog covering the release highlights for localization and Identity & Access Management (IAM) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408. With this release, we deliver a new local version for public sector and additional features for SAP-delivered local versions. In addition, we offer an improved access to documentation for Identity and Access Management (IAM).

Topics covered in this blog:

Localization

  • Local Version for Public Sector
  • Enhancements for Existing SAP-Delivered Versions

Identity and Access Management (IAM)

  • Unified Access to Documentation About Identity and Access Management on SAP Help Portal

 

Localization

Local Version for Public Sector

With the new release, we add a new local industry version for Public Sector including government budget and grants management, fund accounting, and core scope items for finance, procurement, logistics, asset accounting, treasury, leasing, sales, plant maintenance, contract account, and project management.

Value Proposition

Support of public sector users with public cloud solution specific for Germany, Canada, Australia, the Netherlands, and the United Kingdom.

Capabilities

The new scope items in solution scenario SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Public Sector are 6YU, 7AU, 71S, 78S, 6UC, 6R3, 6X0.

Note

For Germany and Canada, an initial version was already delivered with release 2402. New scope items as per above are planned to be added.

For the US all customers not needing GASB accounting principles should also use this version with 2408. These customers shall not use the separate Public Sector edition for the US SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud for public sector any more.

 

Enhancements of Existing Local Versions

Local Versions

Local versions help you meet local requirements by taking into account diverse legal frameworks, and different languages, and by complying with a wide range of local financial reporting standards, tax calculations and laws.

​Value Proposition​

  • Steer business operations globally with ease utilizing SAP-delivered localizations​
  • Address increasing legal changes and compliance requirements on time​
  • Leverage standard processes and automation to address regulatory requirements worldwide, like taxes, e-invoicing, statutory reporting, and payments
New Capabilities for Existing SAP-Delivered Local Versions

Chile

  • Purchase register
  • Sales register

Greece

  • Accounting documents report / document journals

Japan

  • Enhancement of the monthly invoice printing form
  • VAT return to include contract accounting documents

Oman

  • Withholding tax declaration

Peru

  • Inventory and balance ledger for statutory reporting

Serbia

  • E-invoicing for incoming invoices
  • Journal entries audit files

Singapore

  • Payment items approval
  • Cash flow statement

Slovenia

  • Acquisition tax accrual solution
  • BST, KRD and SFT reports
  • Deferred tax

Spain

  • Enablement of SEPA formats for 2024

Switzerland

  • Implementation of a dunning letter
  • Cash flow statement

Ukraine

  • Balance sheet, profit and loss, and cash flow statements
  • Output print forms for logistics

United Kingdom

  • Direct-debit synchronization, notification of mandate changes, and SAP Fiori app for mandate
  • Intercompany settlement in convergent invoicing

 

Identity and Access Management (IAM)

Unified Access to Documentation About Identity and Access Management on SAP Help Portal

Unlock the full potential of SAP S/4HANA Cloud with the unified access to documentation about Identity and Access Management on SAP Help Portal. With user and identity management, roles and authorization, and authentication, you can control and specify user access to apps, ensuring regulatory compliance and security standards. Familiarize yourself with the key concepts and apps to streamline implementation along the different phases and maximize efficiency.

 

Value Proposition 

  • One-stop shop for identity and access management (IAM) documentation
  • Documentation of tasks related to IAM for the different implementation phases (prepare, realize, run, and upgrade) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and integrated products
  • Overview of apps for user and identity management, roles and authorization, and authentication

 

Capabilities 

  • Support users in each implementation phase with dedicated IAM documentation
  • Provide a holistic view on IAM for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and integrated products from initial system access to tasks that need to be completed before, during, and after the upgrade of SAP S/4HANA Cloud solutions
  • Gain an overview of different identity management scenarios

For more information, see Identity and Access Management

 

 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X.

 

️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series! 

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a recording and a presentation for GRC, Trade and Tax as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand – anywhere, anytime. 

 

Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Preliminary What’s New Information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.2
entry.content:
Preliminary versions of What’s New information and of the Product Assistance are now available for customers and partners to get a preview on the upcoming new and changed features of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.2.

You can find this preview on the current Help Portal page SAP S/4HANA Cloud (see What’s New section “Preview of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408.2”). To access the Preliminary What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer or the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The RASD tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The preliminary information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408.2 will be available in RASD within the next few days. We recommend using RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you. A valid S-User ID is needed to use the RASD tool and to view all the preliminary content stated above.

In this release, try the new Commenting Feature for What's New documentation. See this blog for more information.

entry.title: Two Tier ERP Transactional Data Replication options
entry.content:
Two Tier ERP Integration:

Integration is crucial for Two-Tier ERP scenarios, as understanding end-to-end business processes, including both master and transactional data flows, is vital for customer success. It’s important to identify integration requirements early in the process. SAP S/4HANA Cloud will also need to integrate with various other solutions within the customer’s system landscape. Two-Tier ERP offers multiple deployment options, For instance, let's look into one of the deployement option where companies running their main ERP systems (headquarters) on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition or SAP S/4HANA on-premise can connect to subsidiaries using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition.

Two Tier ERP Transactional Data Integration options

For easier understanding, we have separated integration into two categories: Master Data and Transactional Data. This blog focuses on the options for transactional data integration, while you can find the master data options in this linked blog https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/two-tier-erp-master-data-replication-options/ba-p/13879970

Option 1: BTP Integration Suite - Cloud Integration

Top 5 reasons to choose SAP Integration Suite

  1. Tackle ANY integration challenge with a holistic approach.
  2. Accelerate business outcomes with out-of-the-box integration.
  3. Innovate faster with an open API platform for the future.
  4. Speed time to value.
  5. Outperform with enterprise-grade integration.

The Integration Suite is applicable in the following Two-Tier use cases:

  • Headquarters is using S/4HANA on-premise, S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, while subsidiaries are on S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
  • The customer has a mixed landscape, including non-SAP solutions that need to integrate with headquarters.
  • The customer is currently using SAP S/4HANA Cloud editions and plans to expand to additional applications in the future.
  • The customer already has a Business Technology Platform (BTP) account with the Integration Suite.

Key Aspects of SAP Integration Suite from a Two-Tier ERP Integration Perspective:

  • SAP Cloud Integration enables seamless connections between both cloud and on-premise systems, whether they are SAP or non-SAP.
  • It supports real-time scenarios and provides a wide range of pre-built integration content (Integration Packages), along with extensive capabilities and connectivity options.
  • Integration Flows (iFlows) allow you to design message processing logic and configure sender/receiver adapters within SAP Cloud Integration.

You can find details of one of the Two-Tier ERP Buyer and Supplier scenario integration flow here https://api.sap.com/package/IntegrationofSAPS4HANACloudProcurementwithOnPremiseSuppliersS4HANAorERP/integrationflow

Option 2: SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Don’t have an SAP BTP account with the Integration Suite service? No worries—you can still meet your integration needs through the API Hub.

  • Seamlessly discover content that addresses all your integration and extension requirements.
  • Explore integrations between SAP products as well as between SAP and third-party applications.
  • Browse various products, categories, business processes, partners, featured APIs, integrations, and more here https://api.sap.com/search?searchterm=Two%20Tier&tab=All&$refinedBy=true

The key takeaway of this blog is our recommendation to use the SAP Integration Suite service for transactional data replication wherever applicable, allowing you to leverage pre-built integration content for your scenarios.

Future Exploration
In upcoming blogs, we will explore Master Data and Transactional Data in greater detail, and discuss how they can benefit your business.
